Hi, this is Jordan Belfort and welcome to StraightLine Persuasion. I'm really, really excited about this product. If there's one skill in my life that's really allowed me to soar to the highest level financially, it's the ability to influence and persuade people, to be a closer, so to speak. And I've taught this skill to over 12,000 or 13,000 people over the years. And in just about every business you can imagine, starting with Wall Street, all the way to Main Street, and just from the internet to real estate to anything you can imagine. And if this one skill of persuasion is so impactful on people's lives, you have to ask yourself, why is it that so often we do everything that we need to become successful? We learn all the tools and strategies, yet we don't teach ourselves how to actually make our concepts and our visions and our thoughts known to another person. How do you actually influence somebody to want to do business with you? How do you essentially close the sale, not just a product sale, but for information, for your vision, whether you're in a relationship with your spouse trying to get your kids to make your bed? There's a force inside you, this force that once it's unleashed, it allows you to really make your thoughts known in a very, very compelling way. Now my journey has been a rocky one along the way, because when I developed this skill in the beginning, I didn't really pin it with an ethical undertone. It was about making as much money as you can, as fast as you can. And I will tell you this, it was unstoppable. The average age of the person working for me was in their early 20s. The average IQ, wasn't up there in the stratosphere, it was just average people and sometimes below average. But what you have here is a system. A system that allows any person, regardless of their age, race, creed color, socioeconomic background, educational status, it allows any person to master the art of persuasion, to actually close the deal versus talking about closing the deal. And in the end of the day, that's what it's all about. So now I broke this system up here into 10 distinct modules. And each module is extremely important. So you can do this now in a couple of different ways. You can do one module a day for 10 days, which is what I really recommend. Or if you're short of time, you can really spread it out over 10 weeks. Although my experience with that is that people tend to get a little bit less out of it because you lose the continuity of it all. I structured the modules in a specific order, a syntax, as we say, to make sure that you can let one skill build upon the next. And each skill by itself is crucial to your ability to persuade and influence. But when taken together as a system, you'll be shocked at how effective you become. Another option is you can actually do the whole 10 modules in one weekend. You lock the door, you know, you make sure there's no interruptions, and you actually master this. And you're going to get a workbook that comes along with each DVD. And I want you to make notes in this workbook and use it as a way to reinforce what you're learning because there's different ways that we learn. You're actually going to learn something very powerful here in terms of how you process information, how you store information, how you retrieve the information. And my goal here, and I've done this thousands and thousands of times, is to take you and someone like you and to make you what's called unconsciously competent with your ability to persuade and influence. That means you could do it without thinking about it. And when you're doing that, when you're actually in tune with what's in here, not just up here, you're selling from your heart and influencing from your heart, not only you'll be even more effective, but you're going to learn to enjoy it. Now, I know some of you are saying, enjoy persuasion. I hate persuasion. I understand that. And the reason is, is because you just don't know what it really means. Persuading is not about getting people to do things they should don't want to do or shouldn't do. It's about allowing people to overcome the obstacles that are holding them back from taking action, actions they should be taking. And these people who are shut down for whatever reason, whether it's their beliefs about buying, whether it's a fear that if they do take action and don't succeed, they'll look stupid, whatever it might be, your ability, once you learn this system, the straight line system, to help people overcome those obstacles and barriers is going to be so empowering to them that they're going to really just be very, very grateful that they did take action. So I want you to feel confident that once you master this skill, it's going to change your life on every level and also with the way you talk to yourself. You see, we have this voice in the back of our head that very often doesn't serve us and sometimes destroys us. It stops us from taking action. And when you learn this system here, the straight line system, you're going to find that voice in your head, makes a bit of a shift. And you come from this place of lack of confidence to total confidence. You come from a place of uncertainty to a place of certainty. And most importantly, you come from a place where being disempowered is a thing of the past. And empowerment on a very high level is where you live day to day. That's my goal when I created this program. And ironically, when I first was out there teaching the straight line, mostly in the corporate world, I didn't think I'd ever really make a program out of it. But the demand was huge. I get people, where's the straight line? Can we take it home with us? So I created the product for that precise reason. So I would ask you to do this. I want you to really just take this very seriously because it is the single most powerful and important skill that can take you to a whole new level in your business. Whether you own your own business, you're an employee. It's going to be the skill that defines you, that allows you to move up the ladder as an employee or move up the corporate ladder as an owner and make massive sums of money without nearly as much effort as you thought. So I want you to enjoy the program right now. We're going to start off. The first module is basically an introduction to the straight line. And you're going to learn the basic tenets of straight line persuasion, starting with your ability to control the sale. How do you actually take somebody who doesn't really know you and establish some very core principles of being an expert, of having them see value in your product or your idea? And also the fact that whatever company you represent, if it's your own company, how to get the right perception of what your company represents. They trust your company. Does your company have integrity? You're going to learn in a very short period of time through things like tonality, through body language, and also through distinct language patterns, how to create those powerful elements that allow you to persuade elegantly and effectively. But when I get in front of people and we have that exchange of energy, it really allows me to soar to a whole new level of certainty, to access information in my own mind that goes back, you know, 20, 30 years. I'll take any one of you and make you an absolute killer on the phone in person. It doesn't matter. That's what the straight line does. And when I invented it back in 1988, I had the greatest intentions. I really did. I wanted to take a young kid that was 18, 19 years old with zero sales experience. And my goal was to take them, make them into killers. And I came up with this visual. You want to take a look at it. And this is the straight line. It's as simple as that. And what it basically is, it's goal oriented selling. Rather than, you know, when I walk into a sales force and I train corporations for selling 99% of the time when I walk into a company, they hand me like their pitch sheet or whatever it might be. They don't have like lead sheets or their presentation sheets. And usually it looks like just a giant sheet of paper with lots of words on it. And you know, if the customer says this, you do this and it's this giant blob and a morphous blob. And usually what I'll do to get their attention is I'll light it on fire. I'll just light the thing on fire. See, this is the biggest piece of shit. Excuse my language I've ever seen in my life. Because most of the time the scripts that you see out there, what they force a salesman to do is go around in circles. And there's no actual goal oriented start. And a finish. And that's the real key to selling. It doesn't just apply to selling. It's in all situations of communication. Whether it's with your spouse and I caution you when you learn some of this stuff, some of it could be really manipulative with your spouse. So don't use it for that. You know, use it to really for the right reasons. See, here's what the right reason is. It's number one to get people to buy things that they should buy. To overcome obstacles to buy that they should have. Because we all have these buying obstacles and there are these limiting beliefs we have about buying and making decisions and they hold us back from taking action. So what the straight line really does is a very regimented way to get someone which this is called the promise lend here, right? Where they give you the credit card number, they give you cash, whatever it might be, whatever you're selling. I know you're selling a lot of different stuff here, different products, right? And this is where you start right here. Okay. And what's going to happen here is you're going to learn to know these dotted lines that are right here on either side of your sale. And you're going to learn to become masters of controlling every linguistic encounter. And that's the key to the straight line. What it allows you to do is before you start, before you make any sale, I don't care what you're selling. If it's cars, stocks, credit repair, debt settlement, subscriptions to a magazine, memberships to a club, seminars, it doesn't matter. Every sale is the same. And I use it, well, wait a second, every sale is not the same, right? They say different stuff and people have different objections. I get that. What I'm saying is that you're going to learn to take every person's objections and manage them in a way that empowers them, but that also allows you to keep control of the situation, of the sale. Because what happens is once they take control of the sale, you're done. And those of you who are experienced, you kind of know that. The key to selling is to maintain control of the sales. I want to give you some background into sort of what some of the stuff you might have taken sales courses in the past. And I wanted to kind of demystify some things about what selling really is. Then you have 30% where you'll be actually presenting and 40% closing. And the example of this is like, give us the movie Tin Men, where like they're these door to door salesman. They go in for a couple of seconds. They drop a $5 bill on the floor. I said, oh, is this your money to, you know, get credibility with the people. They develop report like that. And they go and give a quick couple of questions they can afford and they slam the presentation and bam, they close, close, close until either the person says no, or they just hit him over the head to say yes. That's the old selling model. And this model's got a bad rap. Okay. If you say to someone, oh, I sell them a closer, it has some stigma to it. So they came up with a new model, which I think is a total joke. Okay. Here's the new model. Okay. Right here. Okay. And what this is, this called consultative selling. And this is selling for people that don't want to admit they're selling. They're like, I'm not really selling anybody. I'm just filling people's needs by asking them what they want. So what happens is this most of the time is spent building relationships or developing rapport, 40% of the time, right? 30% of the time, right? You're identifying what their needs are. Then you present solutions 20% of the time. And then you close 10% of the time. So you really never even closing just a little sliver. Right. This is the biggest crop I should have ever seen in my life. Okay. And I'll tell you why. Okay. And you're in sales, right? If you're not closing, you got to get out of the business. Okay. You know, when you're closing starts and sales, I'll tell you exactly when it starts. Starts right here. The instant you open up your mouth, you're closing the sale. Been bankrupt. Had business I built up, didn't work. Right. So what happens in life is this, there are certain windows that you have when things are working. And when you have that, you got to work twice as hard. It's like a window to jump through opportunity. Right. That's the way it is. So what I want you to do is right now when you're first learning the system, don't say, okay, well, it really works. I'm just going to kind of do what I always did. Anyone know what a widget is? Anyone know? It's a mythical product. What's a widget? Right. That's the question. Okay. We were in school. They taught us about widgets and economics. What a widget is, is you don't, if you want to make a model of something, what you do is you come up with a basic overall model. It doesn't really matter what the product is, but you can then take the product through all layers of production, through producing it, through distributing it, selling it, marketing it, right. The old widget. Okay. And it's for some product that's really, really difficult to sell. So what we're going to be doing for the next two days, is I want you to pretend right now you're selling not what you're selling day to day. You're selling a generic product. And I'm going to show you how to sell a generic product over the phone. Okay. And it's a $4,000 product, $4,000 product to people that you don't know, that you've never spoken to before. And basically you're going to have four seconds to capture their imagination and one call to close the sale. Now, just so you know, the reason I picked this kind of product, it's like being trained to be a Navy SEAL. Right? Once you go through this rigorous training, everything else seems pretty easy, right? So my goal is to show you how to sell something right now that is really, really hard to sell. And then you'll say, geez, wow, I can't sell that. So when you go back to selling whatever it is, a $90 product to get them into a funnel or a $300, whatever it might be, it's going to seem really, really easy. Because the techniques to sell, the techniques to sell, it doesn't matter. You can be selling anything. Okay. So we're going to pick a product. What it is, is the seminar actually. And it's a training that I did in England, where I was training this company and I walked in and it was the worst sales force in history. I'm dysfunctional on every level. The manager was taking, was ripping off the board. So he was like kicking back and like looking at the racing charts. The manager, okay. The salespeople were running hard, wild. They were closing, I think, 30 sales a month, 30 sales a month. And they had the worst scripts ever. And I went in and I trained them for two and a half days. And the next month, that month, in that month, they closed 112 sales. They went 400%. And just that, that in England, it was three weeks. And now I did the company triple, up 300% over six months by switching from what they were doing, which was crap, to the straight line. And I wrote every script for them. I showed them how to manage the sale, the whole nine yards. Okay. So we're going to create, we're going to take that product, the seminar company. Okay. Just to give you some basis here to insert yourself in. So what I wanted to do is please play full out though, because I'm going to actually show you some very powerful stuff with tonal patterns. So it's very important that when you're going through this to really ignite a certain, see what happens is this. We all have massive talent within us. And sometimes you have one of those days, right? We are really, really on. And sometimes you're really, really off. You go, you got, can't believe I said that? I can't believe I did that. I'm such an idiot, right? We have those moments. We just can't close the door. Other days, we just on the money. Everything we say is perfect. My goal is that I want to show you how to ignite those feelings, those emotions from when you're really, really on and show you what it was that you were doing on those particular days. The way you were speaking, the way you were moving, the patterns you, the language patterns you were running. So we're going to do that. Well, I'm going to actually make you sell right in the room. So you actually feel it. It's very powerful. Okay. So real good. Here's the system. Okay. Straight line system. Okay. It's goal oriented versus random conversations. That's the key. Okay. You're not picking up a phone or going face to face for your health. You're there for one reason to close. I'll tell you, I have a son. He's the greatest kid in the world, right? A great soccer player. It's fabulous. He's 14 years old. Since the time he was nine, he was an unbelievable soccer player. And I watch him play, but for some reason he never wanted to score. I was like, look at the fucking goal cause. He's called, look at the goal. Just look at the goal. And it wasn't what he was doing. He was a great playmaker and a passer and that's great. And I love watching him play, but he wasn't goal oriented. He wasn't looking at the promised land. What you have to do as a salesperson is every time you go into an encounter, you have to understand what your outcome is. And your outcome is this, to close. You're not there for your health. You're not there for the other person's health. You are there to close. It is as simple as that. And that one distinction, by the way, when you walk into a room to understand why you're really there, not to make friends. Listen, I'm sure they're nice people that you're selling, right? They're great people, but we all have a lot of friends that are looking for new ones. Okay, you're there to make money. You're there to close. Okay, so this is goal oriented selling. Okay. There are three tenants to the straight line that you need to know. Very, very basic. Number one, you're going to develop instant rapport on both a conscious and an unconscious level. I'm going to show you how to do that. More powerful is the unconscious level. And just so you know what rapport is not. The biggest mistake I see novice sales people make when they go to meet somebody, if they walk into an office and there's a fish on the wall, they'll talk about the fish. No one cares about the damn fish. Okay. When you go into someone's office, developing rapport is not about just playing into what their life is all about. He says he was in a hunting trip in North Carolina or South Carolina. You don't say, Oh, really? Tell me all about the hunting trip. Next thing you know, you're talking about crocodiles and alligators, whatever else is in the Carolina's, right? And what's going on in this hunting trip. That's not how you develop rapport with somebody. You develop rapport with someone when they realize that you're an expert instantly. When they realize that you're a person that could help them achieve goals instantly. That's how you develop rapport. You develop rapport when they know you care. Not about their stupid story. And I'm sure the story's fine, but that's not what makes people want to do business with you. It's when they know that you could help them achieve their goals and most importantly, take control of your life. So number one, you're going to learn in the first four seconds to develop instant unconscious rapport, which will then turn to conscious rapport. Number two, okay, you're going to learn to gather intelligence. There are certain specific questions that you need to ask somebody. And they're slightly different based on what you're selling. But the problem with a lot of salespeople is they talk a lot more than they listen. Asking questions, the right way, not any questions. The right questions is crucial to understanding exactly what your clients needs are. So again, I'm not like one of these hard selling people. It's like, just as barrel through. No, no, this is elegant. It is elegant. Okay. But it happens in a very logical way. And each time you do it, what you're doing is this. If you start off here, each thing that you do takes you slightly, slightly down the line. You're always moving forward, pushing forward in your sale rather than just spiraling out of control like most novice salespeople do. And anyone here a sales manager? Anyone manager? Great. We got one, two, three. This is mana from heaven for a sales manager because it allows you to take new people that have no experience and actually show them the concept of the moving a sale forward. So, you know, you can develop rapport and be moving towards the close at the same time. You can answer an objection and be moving towards the close at the same time. Everyone get this? It's not one or that's why I said those things. They're crap. You know, it's not like you say, okay, I'm going to spend 32 seconds developing rapport. 30 done. I'm done developing rapport. You can go to rapport when the guy is writing out is when he signed the order, you're still developing rapport. It never ends. You're still qualifying the guy. Even after he said yes, because then you're trying to find out what his aliveness is and I'll get into that later. So after the sale, you can develop a customer for life. If there is no pie chart of it starts here and the press, it's all the same. Okay. You're starting off here. You're looking to go here. This is the promised land and every single word out of your mouth has to be deliberate. It has to be looking to get you to here to get the guy what he wants so you can get paid. Now understand this. If you're qualifying the guy and your product does not suit his need, what are you going to do? You're going to push him? No. And the encounter. That's the key. In other words, when you're qualifying somebody, you don't want to be pitching unqualified people. You don't want to be slamming unqualified people close. And if your product, by the way, isn't good for them, try to find them one that is sent to your competitor. That's what it takes. That's what the straight found about just slamming anybody close in a candy. I used it that way and I paid the price emotionally with my freedom money. But if you use the straight line, the right way, you will just get filthy rich in a really, really short period of time. Okay. So that's the two is the gather intelligence. Number three is controlling the sale. By keeping it on the line and moving it from here to here. Now, you see I drove drew these two dotted lines above and below. Okay. So let me just explain to you real quick exactly what this means. And then we'll move forward. The straight line represents the perfect sale. That means everything went exactly right. You walked in the door, they loved you or you're on the phone. They said, oh, yeah, everything's great. And you asked them if they were interested. Yes. I'd love to hear. Great. You tell them what your product about. Oh my God. It sounds wonderful. You say, listen, it costs X miles. Great. How do I get started? How often does that happen? Right. Not too often. That's the perfect sale on the line. Okay. What happens more often than not though, okay, is that you try to move down the line and you keep going and he keeps trying to get you off the line out here into Pluto. He doesn't say what you want. He doesn't follow your preconceived notion about what the sale should be. And you're sitting there trying to like, you know, you're like, you know, it's like you put a Dutchman sticking your finger in the dike trying to stop all the leaks, right? Rather than realizing that before that, before you even start the sale, before you walk in the door, before you pick up the phone, before anything starts, you realize that A, your goal is to keep it from here to go to here on the line. He's going to try to take you off the line. So what you need to do is in a very elegant way that empowers him and that empowers you is keep him on the line or close to it. And in these boundaries here, when he's off the line, you're going to do two things. You're going to one develop rapport. And two gather intelligence. That's what happens when you're off the line. When you're on the line and everything is going as he should. When you're talking, he's saying, aha, and he says, great, how much is a course? You say X, he says, great, closed. That's the exception to the rule. They'd be one out of a hundred sales. Is that, is that simple? So what really happens in the real world is as you're looking to take him down the line, he's looking to push you off the line. So what you're going to learn is some very specific strategies that allow you to stop him from going too far because this is the control. You're here. You're in control. If you're here, you're out of control and you've lost the guy. You're out of rapport. He's off the sale and you're losing time and money and you're wasting your own time. You can't close the guy when he's out here. Okay. When he's here, you can't close him either. You can close the guy when he's between here and here. So you're going to learn strategies that are really, really effective and make him love you. Or her love you is to stop them, move them forward and bring them back to the line. Okay. And that's really the essence, the structure of the straight line through becoming what's called unconsciously competent. There were four levels of learning. Okay. Let me tell you what the levels are. Some of you might know, if you've been through any self development, this is some very basic stuff, but it's important here. The first level is called being unconsciously incompetent. And what that means is this, when you first start doing something, you know, so little about it, you basically know nothing. You don't know what you don't know. This is a very scary spot to be in when you're thrown into a situation and you're a complete novice, you said, geez, I love to get better at this, but I haven't the slightest idea of what I don't know. Everyone follow that concept. This is what most people start off. Okay. The next level is becoming consciously incompetent. That means that you're like, Jesus Christ, I don't know anything. You're like, I did this, does that, does that, is all these strategies, they all sell it. I don't know them. That's the next level. Next level above that is called being consciously competent. Consciously competent means that you're good at something, but you can't really do it with your eyes closed yet. You're good, but it requires all your focus and your mental energy. Like remember when we were kids growing up, you only had to tie your shoe in the beginning. You're like, tie your shoe, like you're putting your things across and so on. You're like, excuse me, don't interrupt me. You can't tie your shoe and have a conversation at the same time. Right. That's when you're consciously competent. It requires all your conscious focus. The next level above that is called being unconscious competence. And that means when you're really, really great at something, you can do it without thinking about it. And the only way to get from conscious competence to unconscious competence is through practice. There's no other way. By drilling it into your head again and again and again and again and again and again until your brain just clicks and all of a sudden using the unconscious part of your mind, which is infinitely more powerful than your conscious mind. Your conscious mind is maybe 10%. Your unconscious mind 90%. It's like 10 times more powerful. So it's a dramatic difference. You get to get there through forced repetition by practicing, by just going out and doing it in one day. All of a sudden click, you'll be unconsciously competent at it. It takes about three or four weeks of really, really trying to get unconsciously competent unconsciously competent at something. So it doesn't happen overnight, but don't worry. Even when you're consciously competent, you'll still be doing great. That's when you'll get a quick bump of 30 to 50%. Right when you leave here in your sales, but about a month later, you'll double your sales if you're working. That's how powerful the system is. Okay. I want to give you an example here. Everyone knows this is a stop sign, right? Okay. Now stop sign is what color everybody? Red, right? Okay. And how about a yield sign? Right? What calls a yield sign? Yellow, right? Okay. That's a yield sign is yellow, right? Okay. Guess what? For 10 years, yield signs have been red. There are no yellow yield signs. There are none. Why do we all think they're yellow? Okay. An unconscious mind made a picture of yellow. They're not yellow. I promise go out there. You'll see today they're all red. They changed them 10 years ago. I went, my son's, I was blown away. I'm like, Jesus Christ. I thought they're all, I look at them. I see yellow. Okay. That's how powerful your unconscious mind is. It rules your conscious mind. We have these triggers that we bypass to save time in the world. Okay. We run patterns in our head. You have a pattern that says when I see a sign triangular, it's a, it's, it's a yield sign, just assume it's yellow because it was when you were a kid. If you ask someone that's younger, they'll say it's red. Like my, it's red. My thought is red. Cause you learn to drive with red yield signs. Right. You go by when you think they're yellow. Okay. You're going to learn, you know, you're going to learn to use this basic strategy to get people to buy. Okay. You're going to learn to bypass people's conscious blocks to get to the unconscious and they'll buy. Okay. So here's the deal. We're going to set goals today. We're all going to set goals, but the next two days is about tactics. I know you've probably been to some seminars, right? And I'm going to tell you, go out and set 30 day goals, 60 day goals, nine, all this sort of stuff, right? And they don't actually tell you how you're going to achieve them. Okay. I'm going to give you, this is all going to be pure tactic. We're going to learn actual real world strategy. If you haven't figured out, you saw the video, you know, a little bit about me. I'm not a theoretical sort of guy. I'm just not. I don't come from the seminar world. I come from the real world and I come from a special corner of the real world. My corner of the real world, everybody's rich. I come from a world of abundance, not scarcity. I come from a world where people that make a million bucks a year are considered like, you know, average. Seriously, that's Wall Street. That's where I came from. You know, you hear about all these wacky bonuses and all this sort of stuff, right? So how do they make all this money? Okay. Well, I'll tell you this at the heart of Wall Street is sales. Stock brokers are just glorified salesmen. Venture capitalists are salesmen. Investment bankers are salesmen. Right, a lot of you, right? The most powerful skill to business is to raise money. Anything for ventures, for what not, whatever you need to talk to suppliers to get credit. But don't really use it for that unless you really want to. Okay. But seriously, try to keep it for business. Okay. You know, you can use it though to get yourself dates. Okay. It's about tactics. Okay. Okay. I told you the most serious work, right? Okay. Now, here's something I want to touch on very, very briefly. Okay. When you set goals in life, okay, your brain, you have the ability, and I'm going to show you how to do this tomorrow. You have the ability to use what's called your neuropsychology to make the goals much more powerful. Okay. To make your goals bigger and brighter. To expand on them. Okay. When I ask you to set goals, right, everybody, I want you to all raise your hand as, raise your hand as high as you can. Everyone raise your right hand as high as you can. As high as you can. Okay. Now raise it a little bit higher. Okay. If I said raise it as high as your hand, and I said raise it, raise it even higher, right? Right. I said the first time, raise it as high as you can, but you didn't. Right. Because it's a level above what's comfortable, slightly higher and easily doable. But you've got to stretch a bit. That's where I want you to set your goals for selling with the straight line, slightly above what you think is great. Not what you think is great, because you can blow that away. I don't want you to set it flying in the sky because you'll say, I can't achieve it. It's too far out in the distance. So we're going to learn to set goals just above what you think is possible. Okay. And you learn to make the goals bigger and brighter using visual technology in your head, auditory, kinesthetic, what you feel. And you're going to blow these goals up. You're going to step inside these goals. Okay. Meaning you get associated with your goal and you can learn to run what's called positive thinking language patterns. Okay. This is called self talk. The first person you have to sell when you go out into the field of soup, bingo yourself, the first person you're going to sell is you on the system that it works. On the job that you have, whatever sales job you have. If you're in sales, by the way, you know you're in business for yourself, right? Everyone know that. When you're in your sales person, you're in business for yourself and you've got to treat it like your own business. Okay. So you learn to run positive language patterns. Okay. And you got to find your why. We're going to do all these three things by the close of business today. Okay. I'm going to show you some patterns to run, to empower yourself, to get yourself into a peak state anytime you want. And especially right before you walk in the door and right before you pick up the phone. Okay. And lastly, you have to shed any programming that you have about sales being evil. You know, wipe it out of your brain. Sales is the most empowering profession in the world. It's a noble profession. Nothing happens in the world without sales. Nothing. Bill Gates, Bill Gates is the best salesman in the world. He is a master. This guy, you know, he started Microsoft. He sold a company called Altair Computers to have by his program, which he did not have. He did not have the program that became, ultimately became Microsoft Windows. He didn't have it. He sold them and said, shit, now we got to go, now we got to go find it. Right. Then they sold some local guy who had it and they bought it from him on the slide. Like they didn't pay him. Hey, can we buy it? And something bought from him. They delivered. They got the money and transferred the money. He influenced everybody to listen to him as the expert. He sold himself as the expert and the guy who could help them achieve their goals and get what they want. That's how we started Microsoft. Is he a brain? He's brilliant. Is he a hard worker? Yeah. But if there's one skill that defines Bill Gates, besides vision, it's his ability to persuade Bill Gates to one of the greatest sales on the earth. Next, Warren Buffett. Think he's a great sales person? Yes or no? Yes. Right. And he says two things. Number one, the single most valuable skill in his life is the ability to persuade. In fact, he was such an awful persuader and he admits this that 1960 or 61, he took a Dale Carnegie course, which back then was the gold standard. Now it's like, you know, you say someone's name eight times, it doesn't really work anymore. But back in 1960, it did, right? Warren Buffett took a Dale Carnegie course and it changed his life. If you go into Warren Buffett's office, the only diploma he has on his wall is from a Dale Carnegie course. That's it. And the school's nothing up there. It was a single defining moment, the ability to persuade other people. When Warren Buffett buys a company, he's the master persuader. How about this person? Persuader or not? Sales on yes or no? Okay. The greatest marketer of all time, the whole entire award. You know what a herd is? Like a herd to the back of the room, right? She has the whole world as her herd. Oprah Winfrey says something and people just do it. She has these loads of women all over the world that they, you ever see this woman who like for one year did everything Oprah said? Okay. I mean, this is influence or not. She's the greatest salesman on earth Oprah. Is she other things too? Yes. She's humanitarian. She's altruistic. She's brilliant. She works hard. She's a visionary, but she's a persuader. But there's more to it than that. There were other skills required too to really become successful. And one of them, and a big one, is not being scared to fail. Not having fear of failure and not having fear of looking stupid. We have this awful fear as human beings. We don't want to look like fools. Sometimes you got to look foolish in the name of progress. I'll give you another metaphor about my son. And I love he's we're really close. I have a daughter too. I'll show you a picture. She's a good girl. She's 16 years old. She's got a boyfriend. It's a whole mine yard. Okay. My son's 14. He's fabulous. And we're having this conversation about when he plays in sports or when he does something about you go full out or do you not? And I see sometimes in him that when he's out on the field or doing something, he doesn't play full out and it'll have an excuse. Like my leg hurts or my arm hurts or something happened. The coach is like, you know, I'm not going to play full out. I'm not going to play full out. The coach was unfair. Whatever. You know, the referee was unfair and what happens with people is we tend to go out into the world and we look for those sort of excuses about why things aren't the way we want them. And when you do that, you lose all your power in life. When you start focusing on the external on the excuses, you lose your power. Here is the way people really think. I will tell you, I can go out on the stage and bomb. I respect you for going out there and trying your hardest. It's not how you actually perform that earns you the respect of your peers. It's the fact that you went out and actually did it. That's the difference. So if you're out there right now and you're a new salesperson or you're struggling as a salesperson, there's no embarrassment in the struggle. It's just not. The embarrassment is in quitting. The embarrassment is in not trying your hardest. And I was trying to explain the distinction to my son because I know inside he feels like everyone is a karate, he's a great player. So he thinks that maybe he didn't live up to our expectations and in some way that makes him not feel good inside. And I'm explaining, this is the hardest thing to learn in life, but you have to remember that all people care about is how much heart you have when you go about doing something. So as a salesperson, salesman, saleswoman, it's about your heart. You're going at it with everything from here up internally or just going through the motions and blaming your boss, blaming the lead source. You can always blame the lead source. You can blame the economy. There's a million reasons why you're not selling as much as you could. Things aren't going the way you want them. They're all excuses that I call it. It's your story. And we have that story. It's like a circuit breaker for fear. We come up with a story of why things aren't the way we want them and we hold onto that story and it stops us from moving forward. In truth, here's what it is. The only thing that stops you from getting what you want in life is the bullshit story you tell yourself of why you can't have it. Get that. What stops you back is the story because the story stops you from getting honest. And if you can't get honest with yourself, there's no way you can go. Imagine like this. You're just in sales and life. It's like a roadmap. You know where you want to go and you have this perfect strategy, the straight line to get there. It's a perfect strategy. It's going to get you there every single time. But if you don't know your starting point, if you're not getting honest with where you actually are, it doesn't matter how powerful the strategy is and it doesn't matter how powerful your goal is. I'm going to ask you to set a goal today, a 60-day goal. And let's say you really set this goal and I'll show you how to do it in a brilliant way. So it's visceral. It's you really feel it. You see it. You sense it. You smell the goal. That's step one. I'm going to teach you the straight line, which is your strategy to get to your goal. But if you're not honest with where you are right now, your starting point, the map is worthless. There's three things that you need to know. Where you are, where you want to go, and how you're going to get there. Those are the three things you must have to take advantage of any opportunity. I'm going to give you the strategy. I'm going to ask you to tell me exactly where you want to go to yourself. You got to get honest. Don't blame the boss. Don't blame the leads. Don't blame your job. Don't blame the industry you're in. Just get honest. If you don't like your industry, find another one. Selling, selling. You can sell anything. You can sell one product, you can sell anything. Okay? So that's the bottom. Oprah Winfrey, another killer, right? Sales person. Next. Two types of people. Okay? And this is great for sales people. Okay? Those who get everything they want in life. Those people are influencers. They get a vision in their mind. They step into that vision and they go for it. And they get what they want in life. As simple as that. That group is 5% of the world. And those are rich people. Very simply. Successful people. The people that get what they want in life. They live life in power. Then you have the other 95% who get nothing. They are creatures of circumstances. You know what a creature of circumstance is? It's someone that goes through life thinking that life happens to them. That's a creature of... You are not creatures of circumstance. Man is a creator of circumstance. You make the world your way. Here's what you do. You see things as they are in the world. Not worse than they are, which is what a lot of people do. They say, oh my God, it's so terrible. The plight is so awful. I could never possibly do anything so they don't take action. They see things worse than they are. You get paralyzed with fear. They say, it's too hard. I can't just no way out. So see things as they are, not worse than they are. And then see them better than they are. And you make them that way. And that's your goal. Your vision for the future. So welcome to the straight line. Glad to have you on board and on the path to wealth and success and to be a master of persuasion and influence. I will tell you this. Maybe you're just a little bit skeptical and I get that. We embrace something new for the first time. We have a tendency that voice in the back of our heads to say it won't work. Maybe it works for everybody else, but not us. And I get that. And what that really is, it's the fact that at this level of learning, you've just become unconsciously competent. Where now you know there's a lot of stuff that you need to know. Before you were unconsciously incompetent, you didn't know what you needed to know. So we're at that second level now. And it's a bit scary sometimes. And I think I've used the metaphor in the past of what a baby does when it learns to walk. Of course, the baby doesn't start to walk on the first couple of tries. Probably takes him 50 or 200 tries. And even when he does walk, he's doing the old Frankenstein walk, but the baby doesn't care. The baby knows it's not supposed to look perfect the first time. But of course, soon enough, the baby's running and it looks perfect. Somewhere along the way, most of us lost that and we think we're supposed to be perfect at things the first time. That's not true. You can make mistakes and people will respect you more afterwards because you overcame those early challenges and embraced something that helped you really soar to a new level of wealth and success. So here is something very important I want to share with you right now. And that's the ability now to leverage what you just learned and bring it out into the real world. So I want you to take two actions right now in the real world based on what you just learned. Not 100 actions, not 50, just two. Something that you saw that made sense to you specifically, I want you to act on it right now. Or tomorrow, if it's nighttime and you go out into the field, whatever you're doing, I want you to learn to take these skills that you've just heard and you visualized, but take them now into the real world. So you've done something very important. You don't know it. But here's what it is. Not only did you commit to taking action by opening up this box and putting the DVD in the player, but you've actually taken action by watching the whole thing going through the workbook. That's huge. So just that alone right now puts you at a much higher level than most people because most people never even take any action. They talk about taking action. They'll write down things about taking action, but they don't ever take action. So I want you to now take parlay, what you just did, and take two actions in the real world, whatever they are. And I want you to don't care how you look if you know you're a little bit lumbering over them. It's perfectly understandable. Pretty soon it'll be elegant. You'll do it second nature. And the second thing I want you to do is I want you to write down what your wise, honest are in life. Why is this important to you? Why do you want to learn to master the art of persuasion and influence? What's it going to give you? Not just money. Let me tell you something. Money, you're going to see once you master the straight line, money will roll in in avalanches of abundance. That's how it is. And just so you know, I've done this with, like I said, 12,000 people over the years. They all became million dollar producers and went on to become very wealthy people in the real world, a variety of businesses. So the money's easy. What I want you to focus on now is the why at a higher level. What is it that you would do with the money? Who are the people that you can help? The people especially you love and can help. Is it your children putting aside money for a college fund? Is it buying a house that you've always dreamed of? Is it retiring your parents? Is it donating to charities that are worthy and that need money? The why's that you have in your life are what really allow you to transcend just being a goal setter to being a visionary, to knowing at a higher level what you're really looking to accomplish, what turns you on here and makes you jump out of bed in the morning. I'll tell you real quickly before I let you go back to the next DVD. When I was in jail and I was at the worst and the lowest point of my life, I had my why's and those were the faces of my children. I knew that if I gave up then I would disappoint my children. I would never be a father for them. They could look up to as a role model and that's what allowed me more than anything was to fight through all that negativity and the boredom and the tediousness of being locked away and actually propelled me to sitting down and writing my first book because I refused to give up because I had my why. I also had a level of excellence that I held myself to, my standards for living. I would not give up. I did whatever it took because I knew my why. Write down your why's tonight. Take those two actions tomorrow and believe me, you'll be glad you did. You'll see huge results. So now let's move on to module two and let's rock and roll. See you soon.
